—The order of Solomon's prayer should be followed. First and most importantly, he prays for repentance and forgiveness, which is the greatest blessing and the only firm foundation for other mercies; then he prays for temporal blessings, teaching us what to focus on and desire in our prayers. Christ has also taught us this in his perfect example of prayer, where there is only one request for outward things, and all the rest are for spiritual blessings. The temple symbolized the human nature of Christ, in whom dwells the fullness of God’s being physically. The ark symbolized his obedience and sufferings, by which repentant sinners have access to a reconciled God and fellowship with him. Jehovah has made our nature his resting-place forever, in the person of Emmanuel, and through him he dwells with and delights in his church of redeemed sinners. May our hearts become his resting-place; may Christ dwell within them by faith, dedicating them as his temples, and spreading abroad his love therein. May the Father look upon us in and through his Anointed; and may he remember and bless us in all things, according to his mercy to sinners, in and through Christ.
